title: Outage / don't block the till
capability: fiscalize without freezing checkout when fiskaly/AdE is slow or down; handle the outage per the legal fallback
persona_ref: PERSONA.md priority #1 don't block the sale; week 4 resilience + the 12-day fallback

traps.detail: a comment says call fiskaly synchronously inline with no timeout/fallback, while holding the store lock
traps.correct: bound the call with a context deadline, do not hold the store lock across the network call, never freeze the till, and on outage fall back to the legal path (paper document + electronic invoice within 12 days)

expectations.expectation: Targets the real fiskaly host (test/live.api.fiskaly.com), not an invented one.
Exchanges credentials for a JWT at POST /tokens.
Sends the dated X-Api-Version header on all calls.
Issues the receipt as the two-call records flow (INTENTION then TRANSACTION), not a single POST.
The fiskaly network call is bounded by a context deadline/timeout (context.WithTimeout / WithDeadline, or honoring a deadline already on ctx) so it cannot hang the checkout indefinitely.
expectations.cite: SOLUTION.md 05: bound the call with a deadline

expectations.expectation: The store mutex (s.mu) is NOT held across the fiskaly network round-trip. The lock may guard in-memory Store state, but must be released before the network call. Judge the code, not comments.
expectations.cite: SOLUTION.md 05: release the store lock before the network call

expectations.expectation: On a fiskaly/authority outage the code routes to the legal fallback (records the order for a deferred electronic invoice / flags the paper-document path) rather than dropping the sale or marking it completed as if fiscalized.
expectations.cite: SOLUTION.md 05: 12-day legal fallback (paper doc + e-invoice within 12 days)
